skill.name: lark-drive
skill.description: Manage files and folders in Lark Drive. Upload and download files, create folders, copy/move/delete files, view file metadata, manage document comments, manage document permissions, and subscribe to user comment change events.
skill.summary: Lark Drive management skill for file operations, folder organization, metadata viewing, comment management, permission control, and event subscriptions.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: All 299 static analysis findings are false positives triggered by markdown documentation syntax. Backticks are markdown code formatting, URLs are documentation examples, and '../' sequences are relative documentation links. The skill uses the official lark-cli tool for legitimate cloud drive operations. Minor risk from external command execution via lark-cli, which requires user authentication.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution via CLI Tool
low_findings.description: The skill executes external commands through the lark-cli binary for cloud drive operations. This is expected behavior for a CLI wrapper skill but requires proper authentication and user authorization.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly requires lark-cli binary and documents all command usage - this is intentional and documented behavior, not a hidden risk.

content.user_title: Manage Lark Drive files and folders
content.value_statement: Streamline your cloud file management in Lark. Upload, download, organize files, manage comments and permissions, all through natural language commands.
actual_capabilities: Upload local files to Lark Drive with custom names
Download files from Lark Drive to local storage
Create, copy, move, and delete folders
Add full-document or selected-text comments to documents
Manage document permissions and collaborate with team members
limitations: Requires lark-cli binary to be installed and authenticated
Wiki links must be resolved to actual document tokens before operations
Local comments (selected text) only supported for docx documents
File uploads via media mode limited to 20MB maximum

use_cases.title: Project Documentation Manager
use_cases.description: Automatically upload project deliverables to shared Lark Drive folders and add review comments for team feedback.

use_cases.title: Research File Organizer
use_cases.description: Download research materials from Lark Drive, organize locally, and upload revised versions with version-controlled comments.

use_cases.title: Team Permission Administrator
use_cases.description: Grant and manage file access permissions for new team members, ensuring proper collaboration setup.
use_cases.target_user: Team leads managing document access control

prompt_templates.title: Upload a file to Lark Drive
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload the file at ./documents/report.pdf to my Lark Drive folder with token fldbc_xxx

prompt_templates.title: Download a file from Lark Drive
prompt_templates.prompt: Download the file with token boxbc_xxx and save it as ./downloads/presentation.pptx

prompt_templates.title: Add a comment to a document
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a comment to the docx document at https://example.larksuite.com/docx/doxcn_xxx saying 'Please review the methodology section'

prompt_templates.title: Manage document permissions
prompt_templates.prompt: Grant full access permission to the uploaded file for the current user, then transfer ownership if confirmed

output_examples.output: File uploaded successfully. File token: boxbc_xxx. You now have full access to this file as an administrator.
File downloaded successfully. Saved to ./output.pdf (1.2 MB)

output_examples.input: Add comment 'Please update the timeline' to docx docxcn_xxx
output_examples.output: Comment added successfully to the document. Comment ID: cmtn_xxx

best_practices: Always verify the document type (docx vs doc) before adding comments, as features differ
For wiki links, resolve to actual document token before performing operations
Confirm user intent before executing write operations like upload, delete, or permission changes
anti_patterns: Do not assume wiki URL tokens are direct file tokens - always resolve first
Do not attempt local comments on non-docx documents - use full-document comments instead
Do not transfer file ownership without explicit user confirmation

faq.question: What is the difference between docx and doc URLs?
faq.answer: Docx is the newer document format with more features. Doc is the legacy format. The skill handles both, but local comments (selected text) only work with docx.

faq.question: How do I handle wiki links?
faq.answer: Wiki links must be resolved first using the wiki.spaces.get_node command to get the actual document token before performing operations.

faq.question: Can I upload files larger than 20MB?
faq.answer: Yes, use the standard upload method. The 20MB limit only applies to media uploads that generate temporary download links.

faq.question: Why can't I add a reply to some comments?
faq.answer: Full-document comments and resolved comments do not support replies. Only unresolved local comments can receive replies.

faq.question: How do I grant access to team members?
faq.answer: Use the permission.members.create command with the user's open_id and specify the desired permission level like full_access.

faq.question: What happens if I lose authentication?
faq.answer: Operations will fail with permission denied errors. Re-authenticate using lark-cli authentication commands before retrying.
